title: trouble adding comcast email to thunderbird - told me it didn't recognize the address
creator: kenneth.lawrence
content: just installed thunderbird on my new windows 11 laptop (samsung galaxy book 5) and tried to add my comcast email account. i entered the address and password and it took me to an xfinity web page to validate my password for imap.comcast.net. since i'm not on my home network it send a code to my mobile phone which i entered successfully. it then asked my for the address on my account. i entered it and it told me it didn't recognize the address! I called comcast and finally got to speak with a human (no mI'veean feat). We verified that my xfinity account allows third party applications to access my email. That's pretty much all the help she could give me. i've run thunderbird on my home windows 10 desktop with no issues accessing my comcast email. Has anyone encountered this problem? does it have to do with windows 11 settings? any help would be appreciated.

title: downloading yahoo mail onto thunderbird
creator: violainecousin
content: Dears since yesterday i cannot download yahoo mails to thunderbird on my windows computer. when thunderbird gets new messages, a window pops up asking me for yahoo account name and password. when entered yahoo gives the following message: " Something went wrong. Try again from a different device." i use thunderbird 148.0 on windows 11 (out of china, with Astrill VPN - all other websites work normally with VPN). i have three different yahoo accounts, the first time thunderbird asked yahoo first mail and authentification went through, but it does not go through for the remaining two accounts. i verified that OAuth2 is set up for all accounts. password is correct (as i can still receive emails on iphone mail app). cookies can be accepted without any exceptions, can see the whole OAuth2 dialog panel. i did all steps for setting multiple accounts. i verified all accounts for POP and STMP server settings. also mailnews.oauth.usePrivateBrowser set to TRUE. title: Notification of new message in Threaded View.
creator: martin388
content:
Hello, In my Mozilla Thunderbird email client I'm sorting received emails into 'conversations' which in Thunderbird is known as 'Threaded View'. I find this to be useful functionality but one problem I have is that it's not obvious when there's a new email in that thread. The only way to check for a new message in a thread, is to click on the '<n> replies' in the very first threaded email, so that when that expands to show all content in that thread - one can then see that there are new emails that haven't yet been read. Is there any way that new (unread) emails in Threaded View can be made obvious ?. Regards, 

 Martin388

title: A solution to  emails keep returning to spam folder...
creator: DonH8888
content:
I had a similar problem. The Spam settings were being picked up by my ISP from my mobile. When I looked at the Spam on my mobile, I could mark each individual email as "Not Spam". When I did this, the email appeared in the Inbox not only of my mobile but of my laptop too. A new test email went straight into Inbox of both devices and not into Spam of either.

I hope this helps.
